data: Iscrizioni latine lapidarie del Museo di Palermo
Livia Bivona, Iscrizioni latine lapidarie del Museo di Palermo, Sikelia 5 (Palermo: Flaccovio, 1970)
A large plaque of grey marble, broken in half vertically and rejoined; damaged on the left and below.

The text maintains a justified left margin, but uneven right margin, with words regularly broken across the line-end, and no attempt to make the end of the text symmetrical or to match the content to the line-length / layout, as opposed to simply a consistent filling of the face until the text is complete.
Tall, relatively narrow letters, with short horizontal strokes often curving upwards (especially on T).
Among the inscriptions originally displayed in the Palazzo delle Aquile; currently on display in the Museum courtyard.
I.Sicily with the permission of the Assessorato Regionale dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana - Dipartimento dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana; photo R. Henzel, 2016-11-07
Partially reopened following extensive restoration work. Comprehensive collection of archaeological material, predominantly from the West of the island, with an important Etruscan collection.
Centre of Palermo, Piazza Olivella 24, 90100 - Palermo

Apparatus criticus

  • Text of ILMusPalermo

English translation

Tuuli Ahlholm

To Tiberius Claudius Herodianus, a most distinguished man (i.e. of senatorial rank), most extraordinary judge, patron of the colony of Panhormitans. The foremost men (of the colony) gave (this monument) as a gift from communally collected bronze.
